title: Combined-interest check with single-bucket subtraction underflows
task_prompt: Check whether withdraw logic correctly handles combined interest balances without underflowing a single accounting bucket.

ground_truth.root_cause: Precondition checks amount against totalInterest + totalInterestFromLiquidation but subtraction is applied only to totalInterest.
ground_truth.exploit_path: When amount exceeds totalInterest but is still <= combined total, subtraction from totalInterest underflows and reverts.
ground_truth.impact: Valid withdrawals can fail, locking otherwise withdrawable interest.
ground_truth.severity_rationale: Core interest-withdrawal path can revert under realistic balance splits, blocking expected treasury operations.
ground_truth.recommended_fix: Subtract proportionally or in sequence across both buckets, or compute available source before mutating each balance.

title: Liquidation wraps ETH without ensuring native balance
public_reference: Minimized reconstruction based on public finding: https://github.com/sherlock-audit/2024-11-autonomint-judging/issues/235
task_prompt: Assess whether liquidation assumes native ETH is already present before attempting WETH wrapping.

ground_truth.root_cause: Function computes an ETH amount and calls weth.deposit{value: amount}() without guaranteeing the contract holds that ETH.
ground_truth.exploit_path: Liquidation path executes with insufficient contract ETH; WETH deposit reverts; liquidation aborts.
ground_truth.impact: Undercollateralized positions may remain unliquidated when liquidation path fails.
ground_truth.severity_rationale: Medium because failure is conditional on funding state, but it blocks critical risk-management operations.
ground_truth.recommended_fix: Ensure ETH funding before wrapping, or source liquidity safely and fail fast with explicit balance checks.

title: Token claim hijack via missing caller-authorization binding
task_prompt: Determine whether claim authorization is bound to the actual caller or can be frontrun by another address.

ground_truth.root_cause: Signature proves authorization for kycAddress, but transfer destination uses msg.sender without requiring msg.sender == kycAddress.
ground_truth.exploit_path: Attacker copies victim claim tx from mempool, submits same signed payload first, receives tokens, and consumes nonce.
ground_truth.impact: Authorized claimant loses tokens while nonce is burned.
ground_truth.severity_rationale: Single frontrunnable call lets attacker steal claimable distribution and deny legitimate redemption.
ground_truth.recommended_fix: Require msg.sender == kycAddress (or transfer to kycAddress) and bind nonce consumption to the intended recipient with replay protection.

title: One-time claim gate blocks future epoch rewards
public_reference: Minimized reconstruction based on public finding: https://github.com/sherlock-audit/2024-10-gamma-rewarder-judging/issues/74
task_prompt: Check whether reward claims across multiple epochs can be completed or become permanently blocked after first claim.

ground_truth.root_cause: Function uses claim.amount == 0 as a one-time gate, preventing later epoch claims once any claim has been recorded.
ground_truth.exploit_path: User claims once in early epoch; subsequent valid proofs revert due to one-time gate despite remaining rewards.
ground_truth.impact: Users cannot access full entitled rewards across epochs.
ground_truth.severity_rationale: Protocol-level accounting mismatch permanently denies claimable rewards for valid participants.
ground_truth.recommended_fix: Track claimed amounts per epoch or cumulative entitlement deltas instead of a single one-time gate flag.

title: Exact-balance assumption breaks fee-on-transfer allocation
public_reference: Sanitized variant based on public finding: https://github.com/sherlock-audit/2024-06-boost-aa-wallet-judging/issues/52
task_prompt: Assess whether token accounting assumes full transfer amount and fails for fee-on-transfer ERC20 tokens.

ground_truth.root_cause: Post-transfer invariant requires exact balance delta == requested amount, which is false for fee-on-transfer tokens.
ground_truth.exploit_path: Caller uses fee-on-transfer token; safeTransferFrom credits less than payload amount; strict balance check reverts.
ground_truth.impact: Allocations fail for supported-looking tokens, causing operational DoS for those assets.
ground_truth.severity_rationale: Medium because funds are not directly stolen, but protocol functionality fails for a realistic token class.
ground_truth.recommended_fix: Use actual received amount for accounting or explicitly reject fee-on-transfer tokens with clear checks/documentation.

title: Refund path forgets to release locked ETH accounting
public_reference: Minimized reconstruction based on public finding: https://github.com/sherlock-audit/2024-08-winnables-raffles-judging/issues/47
task_prompt: Check if refund logic updates locked-funds accounting consistently with outgoing ETH transfers.

ground_truth.root_cause: Locked ETH counter is incremented during purchase but not decremented during refunds.
ground_truth.exploit_path: Repeated refund cycles leave stale locked accounting, eventually making available balance appear lower than real transferable ETH.
ground_truth.impact: ETH becomes effectively stranded by incorrect internal accounting.
ground_truth.severity_rationale: Core treasury/accounting path drifts over time and can lock owner-usable funds.
ground_truth.recommended_fix: Decrement locked accounting by refunded amount inside refund path before/with transfer effects.

title: Unrestricted setter execution resets multisig approval progress
task_prompt: Determine whether a privileged setter execution path can be called by arbitrary users and disrupt approval state.

ground_truth.root_cause: Privileged execution function lacks owner-only guard and mutates shared approval state.
ground_truth.exploit_path: Any address calls executeSetterFunction after partial approvals and clears progress, forcing owners to restart approvals.
ground_truth.impact: Unauthorized users can grief governance operations and delay critical parameter updates.
ground_truth.severity_rationale: Medium due to denial-of-operation and governance disruption without direct fund theft in minimal variant.
ground_truth.recommended_fix: Restrict executeSetterFunction to approved owner set and separate approval reset from externally callable paths.
